definition: A state where fiscal dynamics partially override monetary policy as the dominant macro force, reflected in maturity-dependent correlation bifurcation. The front end still transmits monetary policy intent (negative 2Y-SPX) while the back end transmits fiscal supply pressure (positive 30Y-SPX). Full fiscal dominance would be signaled by 2Y-SPX turning positive.
evidence: $36T+ debt with net interest ~$1T/year approaching post-WWII maximum
0/0 base rate for FFR exceeding 5.5% with debt/GDP >100%
Pause at 5.25-5.50% with core PCE at 4.3% is observable revealed preference
UK Truss episode (2022) provides quasi-experimental confirmation of market-imposed fiscal discipline
Kalecki channel recycles 10-25% of rate-hike contractionary intent as fiscal stimulus to bondholders
Maturity bifurcation between 2Y and 30Y correlation with equities
1994 and 2013 analogues occurred at much lower debt and deficit levels
No credible fiscal consolidation path through at least 2028
Supply pressure is recurring and structural, not a one-time shock
open_questions: Is 2Y-SPX turning positive a reliable signal or could it reflect other dynamics?
At what debt/GDP ratio does fiscal dominance become irreversible?
Is the binding constraint structural (fiscal arithmetic) or political (appointment capture, mandate erosion)?
What are the relative weights of structural vs political drivers?
Does the Truss analogue apply to the US given reserve currency status?
Does garden-variety QT plus issuance sufficiently explain the bifurcation without invoking fiscal dominance?
At what debt/GDP level does fiscal dominance fully capture the long end?
How does partial fiscal dominance interact with Fed independence?
